job.description: Zero-Day Buffer Overflow Fix for Concrypt-Core Storage Module.

strategy.rubric: 1. Patch Verification: Confirm the pull request modifies the affected 'Concrypt-Core Storage Module' logic, replacing unsafe memory operations (e.g., memcpy, sprintf) with bounds-checked alternatives. 2. Exploit Neutralization: Execute the provided Zero-Day Proof of Concept (PoC); the fix is successful only if the PoC no longer triggers a segmentation fault or unauthorized memory access. 3. Regression Suite: Ensure all existing storage module unit tests pass with 100% success rate. 4. Memory Safety: Run Valgrind or a similar memory sanitizer to ensure the fix does not introduce memory leaks or use-after-free vulnerabilities. 5. Cryptographic Proof: Verify via vlayer-skill that the patched binary hash matches the audited source code committed to the repository.
